---
title: "`r params$title`"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
params:
  title: "RNAdetector Long RNA-Seq Analysis Report"
  pipeline_version: "2.0"
  analysis_type: "long_rna"
  algorithm: "star"
  counting_algorithm: "featurecounts"
  de_algorithm: "deseq2"
  genome_name: "Human (hg38)"
  annotation_name: "GENCODE v38"
  threads: 4
  memory_gb: 8
  trim_enabled: true
  trim_quality: 20
  trim_length: 50
  output_dir: "."
  job_name: "long_rna_analysis"
  input_files: ""
  pathway_analysis: false
  circ_rna_detection: false
  circ_algorithm: "ciri2"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 4
    theme: darkly
    highlight: monokai
    code_folding: show
    self_contained: true
    number_sections: true
bibliography: references.bib
csl: nature.csl
---

```{css custom-dark-theme, echo=TRUE}
/* Custom CSS for dark theme report styling */
body {
  font-family: 'Segoe UI', Tahoma, Geneva, Verdana, sans-serif;
  line-height: 1.7;
}
body p {
  text-align: justify;
}
.table-striped > tbody > tr:nth-of-type(odd) {
  background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05);
}
pre {
  border: 1px solid #444;
}
```

```{r setup, echo=TRUE}
# Load required libraries for long RNA-Seq analysis reporting
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(knitr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(DT)
})

# Set global chunk options: all code visible
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 6,
  fig.align = "center"
)

# Set ggplot2 theme for dark background reports
theme_dark_report <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#222222", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#2d2d2d", color = NA),
    text = element_text(color = "#e0e0e0"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "#cccccc"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#444444"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "#333333"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "#222222"),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "#e0e0e0"),
    plot.title = element_text(color = "#ffffff", size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "#bbbbbb")
  )
```

# Analysis Overview

```{r analysis-overview, echo=TRUE}
# Display summary of the long RNA-Seq analysis parameters
cat("**Job Name:**", params$job_name, "\n\n")
cat("**Analysis Type:** Long RNA-Seq\n\n")
cat("**Reference Genome:**", params$genome_name, "\n\n")
cat("**Annotation:**", params$annotation_name, "\n\n")
cat("**Alignment Algorithm:**", params$algorithm, "\n\n")
cat("**Counting Algorithm:**", params$counting_algorithm, "\n\n")
cat("**DE Algorithm:**", ifelse(is.null(params$de_algorithm), "none", params$de_algorithm), "\n\n")
cat("**Pipeline Version:**", params$pipeline_version, "\n\n")
```

# Materials and Methods

```{r materials-methods, child='materials_methods.Rmd', echo=TRUE}
# Include the shared Materials and Methods child document.
# This generates publication-ready methods text based on the params.
```

# Alignment Statistics

```{r alignment-stats, echo=TRUE}
# Read and display alignment statistics from the pipeline output
alignment_stats_file <- file.path(params$output_dir, "alignment_stats.tsv")
alignment_log_file <- file.path(params$output_dir, "alignment_stats.txt")

# Try multiple possible file locations
stats_file <- NULL
for (candidate in c(alignment_stats_file, alignment_log_file)) {
  if (file.exists(candidate)) {
    stats_file <- candidate
    break
  }
}

if (!is.null(stats_file)) {
  # Read alignment statistics
  alignment_data <- read.delim(stats_file, header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  cat("### Alignment Summary\n\n")

  # Display the alignment statistics table
  kable(alignment_data, format = "html", caption = "Alignment Statistics Per Sample") |>
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                              full_width = TRUE)
} else {
  cat("Alignment statistics files not found in output directory.\n")
  cat("Expected location:", alignment_stats_file, "\n")
}
```

```{r alignment-rate-plot, echo=TRUE, fig.cap="Alignment rate (%) per sample. Higher alignment rates indicate better mapping quality."}
# Generate alignment rate bar plot if data is available
if (!is.null(stats_file) && exists("alignment_data")) {
  if ("alignment_rate" %in% colnames(alignment_data) || "Alignment.Rate" %in% colnames(alignment_data)) {
    rate_col <- ifelse("alignment_rate" %in% colnames(alignment_data),
                       "alignment_rate", "Alignment.Rate")
    sample_col <- colnames(alignment_data)[1]

    plot_df <- data.frame(
      Sample = alignment_data[[sample_col]],
      Rate = as.numeric(gsub("%", "", alignment_data[[rate_col]]))
    )

    ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = reorder(Sample, Rate), y = Rate)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#4fc3f7", width = 0.7) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 80, linetype = "dashed", color = "#ff8a65", linewidth = 0.5) +
      coord_flip() +
      labs(
        title = "Alignment Rate per Sample",
        x = "Sample",
        y = "Alignment Rate (%)"
      ) +
      ylim(0, 100) +
      theme_dark_report
  }
}
```

# Gene Expression Analysis

```{r load-counts, echo=TRUE}
# Load the gene expression count matrix from the pipeline output
counts_file <- file.path(params$output_dir, "counts_matrix.tsv")
counts_file_alt <- file.path(params$output_dir, "counts.tsv")

count_data <- NULL
for (candidate in c(counts_file, counts_file_alt)) {
  if (file.exists(candidate)) {
    count_data <- read.delim(candidate, header = TRUE, row.names = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    cat("Count matrix loaded:", nrow(count_data), "genes x", ncol(count_data), "samples\n")
    break
  }
}

if (is.null(count_data)) {
  cat("Count matrix file not found in output directory.\n")
  cat("Expected location:", counts_file, "\n")
}
```

## Expression Distribution

```{r expression-distribution, echo=TRUE, fig.cap="Distribution of log2(counts + 1) across all samples. Density plots reveal the overall expression landscape and help identify potential outlier samples."}
# Plot the distribution of gene expression values across all samples
if (!is.null(count_data)) {
  # Transform counts to log2 scale for visualization
  log_counts <- log2(count_data + 1)

  # Reshape data for plotting
  plot_data <- data.frame(
    Expression = unlist(log_counts),
    Sample = rep(colnames(log_counts), each = nrow(log_counts))
  )

  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Expression, color = Sample)) +
    geom_density(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.8) +
    labs(
      title = "Gene Expression Distribution",
      subtitle = "Log2(counts + 1) per sample",
      x = "Log2(counts + 1)",
      y = "Density"
    ) +
    theme_dark_report
}
```

## Library Size

```{r library-size, echo=TRUE, fig.cap="Total library size (number of mapped reads) per sample. Large differences may indicate technical variability."}
# Plot library sizes (total counts per sample)
if (!is.null(count_data)) {
  lib_sizes <- data.frame(
    Sample = colnames(count_data),
    Total_Counts = colSums(count_data)
  )

  ggplot(lib_sizes, aes(x = reorder(Sample, Total_Counts), y = Total_Counts / 1e6)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#81c784", width = 0.7) +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(
      title = "Library Size per Sample",
      x = "Sample",
      y = "Total Counts (millions)"
    ) +
    theme_dark_report
}
```

## Top Expressed Genes

```{r top-genes-table, echo=TRUE}
# Identify and display the top 30 most highly expressed genes by mean expression
if (!is.null(count_data)) {
  # Calculate mean expression per gene across all samples
  gene_means <- data.frame(
    Gene = rownames(count_data),
    Mean_Count = rowMeans(count_data),
    Median_Count = apply(count_data, 1, median),
    Max_Count = apply(count_data, 1, max),
    Detected_In = rowSums(count_data > 0)
  )

  # Sort by mean expression and take top 30
  top_genes <- head(gene_means[order(-gene_means$Mean_Count), ], 30)
  top_genes$Mean_Count <- round(top_genes$Mean_Count, 2)
  top_genes$Median_Count <- round(top_genes$Median_Count, 2)
  rownames(top_genes) <- NULL

  # Display as interactive table
  datatable(
    top_genes,
    caption = "Top 30 Most Highly Expressed Genes (by Mean Count)",
    options = list(
      pageLength = 15,
      dom = "ftip",
      order = list(list(1, "desc"))
    ),
    rownames = FALSE
  )
}
```

```{r top-genes-barplot, echo=TRUE, fig.cap="Top 20 most highly expressed genes ranked by mean counts across all samples."}
# Barplot of the top 20 most expressed genes
if (!is.null(count_data)) {
  top20 <- head(gene_means[order(-gene_means$Mean_Count), ], 20)

  ggplot(top20, aes(x = reorder(Gene, Mean_Count), y = Mean_Count)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#ffb74d", width = 0.7) +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(
      title = "Top 20 Most Highly Expressed Genes",
      x = "Gene",
      y = "Mean Count"
    ) +
    theme_dark_report
}
```

## Gene Detection Summary

```{r gene-detection, echo=TRUE}
# Summarize gene detection across samples
if (!is.null(count_data)) {
  total_genes <- nrow(count_data)
  detected_genes <- sum(rowSums(count_data) > 0)
  detected_all <- sum(rowSums(count_data > 0) == ncol(count_data))

  cat("**Total genes in annotation:**", total_genes, "\n\n")
  cat("**Genes detected (>0 counts in any sample):**", detected_genes,
      sprintf("(%.1f%%)", 100 * detected_genes / total_genes), "\n\n")
  cat("**Genes detected in all samples:**", detected_all,
      sprintf("(%.1f%%)", 100 * detected_all / total_genes), "\n\n")
}
```

# Software Versions

```{r software-versions, echo=TRUE}
# Compile a table of all software tools and their versions
software_df <- data.frame(
  Software = c("RNAdetector", "R", "Bioconductor"),
  Version = c(
    params$pipeline_version,
    paste(R.version$major, R.version$minor, sep = "."),
    as.character(BiocManager::version())
  ),
  Reference = c("Alaimo et al., 2020", "R Core Team, 2023", "Huber et al., 2015"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Add tool-specific versions based on parameters
if (params$algorithm == "star") {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "STAR", Version = "2.7.9a", Reference = "Dobin et al., 2013"))
} else if (params$algorithm == "hisat2") {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "HISAT2", Version = "2.2.1", Reference = "Kim et al., 2019"))
} else if (params$algorithm == "salmon") {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "Salmon", Version = "1.5.2", Reference = "Patro et al., 2017"))
}

if (params$counting_algorithm == "featurecounts") {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "featureCounts", Version = "2.0.3", Reference = "Liao et al., 2014"))
} else if (params$counting_algorithm == "htseq") {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "HTSeq", Version = "0.13.5", Reference = "Anders et al., 2015"))
} else if (params$counting_algorithm == "stringtie") {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "StringTie", Version = "2.1.7", Reference = "Pertea et al., 2015"))
}

if (params$trim_enabled) {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "Trim Galore", Version = "0.6.6", Reference = "Krueger, 2015"))
}

kable(software_df, format = "html", caption = "Software Versions") |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

# Session Information

```{r session-info, echo=TRUE}
# Print full R session information for complete reproducibility
sessionInfo()
```

# References
